message: Not much. I spent the Sunday at the bike race at the Hot Brakes event. I came in 5th in my age group.
That's really good. I wouldn't mind going for a ride with you. Maybe we can go tomorrow?
Maybe. My mom is mad because my grades aren't very good and has threatened to take my bike away if I don't do better.
That's not fair. You are doing so well with your racing.
Exams are next week, and she's acting if I don't get straight A's, I'm gonna ruin my life or something.
Are those the biology and English exams? I have those too.
Yeah, I'm also behind on a major project in social studies and need to submit an English essay. My teachers gave me extensions but I need to get them done ASAP.
Sounds like a lot all at once.
Yeah. It's not all my fault. I was distracted because I was having problems with my boyfriend, so I got behind.
I'm sorry to hear that. Are things okay with you two now?
Not sure. We are talking, but we still have some things to work out. Now I don't know where to start with my schoolwork.
I've been there. Sometimes everything seems urgent and overwhelming.
Riding my bike helps me destress so its not helpful if I can't ride.

options.text: When I get behind and stressed, I try to make a plan. I write a list of tasks and put the most urgent at the top.
When I get behind and stressed, I feel paralyzed and don't know where to start. I end up distracting  myself with other things.
